threats.scenario: XML file upload via POST /file-upload processes XML with noent:true (routes/fileUpload.ts:83): libxml.parseXml(data, { noent: true }). This enables XXE — attacker can include file:///etc/passwd external entity in XML to exfiltrate server files. Error message includes parsed XML content revealing the exfiltrated data.

remediation.title: Disable external entity resolution in XML parser
steps: Change noent from true to false: libxml.parseXml(data, { noblanks: true, noent: false, nocdata: true })
Also set nonet: true to prevent network entity resolution
Consider rejecting XML uploads entirely if not required

threats.scenario: ZIP file upload in handleZipFileUpload (routes/fileUpload.ts:35-60) extracts files to uploads/complaints/ directory. While absolutePath.includes(path.resolve('.')) check exists, the unzipper 0.9.15 version has known path traversal vulnerabilities in directory traversal handling of symlinks and crafted paths, potentially allowing writes outside uploads/.
threats.controls_in_place: Path resolution check (potentially bypassable with old unzipper version)

remediation.title: Update unzipper to current version; strengthen path traversal check
steps: Update unzipper from 0.9.15 to current version (^0.12.x)
Verify extracted path starts with path.resolve('uploads/complaints/') + path.sep
Use path.normalize() before comparison to handle double slashes

threats.scenario: Profile image URL upload (POST /profile/image/url) authenticates via cookie token (routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:22), but any URL is fetched server-side. The imageUrl is not validated for scheme, host, or content type before fetching. Attacker can make the server fetch internal cloud metadata (http://169.254.169.254/), redis, database, or other internal services.

remediation.title: Implement URL validation before server-side fetch of profile image
steps: Resolve DNS and verify IP is not RFC 1918 or loopback
Validate Content-Type response header is image/*
